deep_dive.title: Diffusion bridge with randomized initial and terminal times and its application to fish migration
deep_dive.abstract: We mathematically model the dynamics of the number of migratory fish observed at a fixed location along a river in a random environment. Particularly, as a new approach, we construct a stochastic differential equation that incorporates the influence of environmental factors on the fluctuations in the start and end of migration. The model is a diffusion bridge with a non-Lipschitz diffusion coefficient, called the Cox-Ingersoll-Ross bridge, and has random initial and terminal times arising from time-change, so that the influences of environmental factors can be efficiently incorporated. The well-posedness of the model is first established, which is considered novel and significant in applied mathematics. Second, we estimate the parameters of the model based on the latest multiyear daily data set for the upstream migration of Plecoglossus altivelis altivelis (Ayu) by relying on the hypothesis that water temperature affects the migration of the fish, which has been suggested in existing studies. We also explore the application of the proposed model to the challenging task of analyzing environmental DNA data. This study advances the development of a theory of fish migration that is simple yet can take environmental factors into account.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Explicit construction of a diffusion bridge (CIR bridge) with random times for fish migration modeling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 明确构造了具有随机时间的扩散桥（CIR桥）用于鱼类迁徙建模
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 明确构造了具有随机时间的扩散桥（CIR桥）用于鱼类迁徙建模
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 明确构造了具有随机时间的扩散桥（CIR桥）用于鱼类迁徙建模
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Constructs a Cox-Ingersoll-Ross diffusion bridge with randomized initial and terminal times, proving well-posedness and applying to fish migration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对环境因素影响下洄游鱼类观测数量动态建模，提出了一种具有随机起始和终止时间的扩散桥模型（CIR桥）。时间变化源自环境因素影响，模型具有非Lipschitz扩散系数。首先建立了模型的适定性，这在应用数学中具有创新性和重要理论意义。然后估计了模型参数并应用于鱼类洄游数据。该工作直接体现了扩散桥在连接两个概率分布中的核心作用，尽管应用背景为生态学，其数学构造对扩散桥理论有直接贡献。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: diffusion bridge connecting two probability distributions

deep_dive.title: Mixture-of-Gaussians-Guided Schedule Design for Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models (BBDM) offer an appealing framework for image restoration and inverse problems by constructing a stochastic bridge from the clean signal directly to the degraded observation, rather than to pure noise. Despite their promise, the choice of bridge schedule is typically inherited from heuristics, and a principled analytical framework for schedule design has been lacking. In this work, we develop such a framework by offering a novel analysis of BBDM reverse dynamics under a Mixture-of-Gaussians (MoG) prior. This setting yields a closed-form ideal posterior and a corresponding MMSE denoiser, while the BBDM-induced reconstruction law is captured analytically through a tractable surrogate. Building on these expressions, we formulate two complementary schedule-design objectives: a Wasserstein criterion targeting perceptual quality and an MSE criterion targeting reconstruction fidelity. Our work exposes an inherent tradeoff between the two and proves the existence of universal schedules for both that are independent of the degradation and prior. Extensive experiments on controlled MoG settings confirm full alignment between theory and practice, and experiments on the FFHQ dataset across inpainting, deblurring, and super-resolution tasks validate the practical value of our schedule-design criteria.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Brownian bridge diffusion models for generative modeling

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a principled schedule design framework for Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models (BBDM) using a Mixture-of-Gaussians prior, enabling closed-form posterior and improved image restoration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对布朗桥扩散模型（BBDM）的调度设计提出原则性分析框架。利用混合高斯先验推导出闭式理想后验和MMSE去噪器，并解析捕捉BBDM重建律。该方法为图像恢复等逆问题提供了理论指导，避免了启发式调度选择，提升了生成质量。

deep_dive.title: Reflected Schrödinger Bridge Matching
deep_dive.abstract: Recent advances in generative modeling have enabled the efficient computation of Schrödinger bridges (SB) in high-dimensional settings by leveraging partially simulation-free training methods inspired by flow matching. However, these have not covered SBs with reflecting dynamics, a useful model choice with built-in guarantees that generated samples stay in the data domain. Existing alternatives for reflected SBs instead rely on more complex training based on forward--backward SDE theory, requiring expensive higher-order derivatives and sampling entire paths during training. In this article, we introduce a partially simulation-free framework that allows reflected SBs to be trained similarly to flow matching, using a new sampling method and regression target. We demonstrate our results by coupling pairs of well-known high-dimensional image datasets. Using reflected dynamics incurs negligible additional wall-clock time during both training and inference while maintaining or slightly improving generative performance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes reflected Schrodinger bridge matching, a partially simulation-free framework
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出反射型薛定谔桥匹配，部分模拟自由训练框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出反射型薛定谔桥匹配，部分模拟自由训练框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出反射型薛定谔桥匹配，部分模拟自由训练框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a partially simulation-free method for reflected Schrodinger bridges, enabling training similar to flow matching with a new sampling method and regression target, demonstrated on generative modeling tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对反射型薛定谔桥提出了一种部分模拟自由的训练框架。现有反射SB需要复杂的前向后向SDE训练，本文借鉴流匹配思想，设计新的采样方法和回归目标，使得反射SB可以像流匹配一样高效训练。实验表明该方法在生成建模中表现优异，同时保证生成样本始终在数据域内。该工作将薛定谔桥匹配扩展到反射边界情形，提升了实际应用的可行性。

deep_dive.title: What Does a Discrete Diffusion Model Learn?
deep_dive.abstract: What does a discrete diffusion model learn: a denoiser, a score ratio, or a bridge plug-in predictor? At the level of jump rates, these are one object in different coordinates, and reading a neural network in the wrong coordinate changes the process being trained and sampled. Starting with a rigorous derivation of the continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC) ELBO for any noising process, boundary terms included, we prove the \emph{Oracle Distance} theorem: the negative ELBO is exactly equal to the data entropy plus the path KL from the oracle reverse process to the learned one, not merely a bound. Its unique optimizer is therefore the conditional expectation of the true reverse jump rate given the current noisy state, and its irreducible cost is the rate at which the forward process $Z_t$ destroys information about the clean data $Z_0$, $-\tfrac{d}{dt}I(Z_0; Z_t)$, so every noising process shares the same best achievable negative ELBO: the data entropy. For sequences with token-factorizing noise, the oracle projection yields three exact coordinates for the optimizer: denoiser, cavity (bridge plug-in), and score, with closed-form conversions among them. This framework identifies which law each loss in the literature actually optimizes, recovering MDM, UDM, SEDD, and GIDD as special cases; explains why denoiser and cavity coincide for masked diffusion but not for uniform diffusion; proves that a denoiser parameterization makes the uniform ELBO diverge at initialization while the bridge plug-in stays finite; and calibrates ELBO implementations exactly at initialization. Every identity is verified numerically, without approximation, on an exactly solvable model.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: explicitly discusses bridge plug-in predictor in discrete diffusion models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 明确讨论了离散扩散模型中的桥接插件预测器
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 明确讨论了离散扩散模型中的桥接插件预测器
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 明确讨论了离散扩散模型中的桥接插件预测器
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proves Oracle Distance theorem for discrete diffusion, showing negative ELBO equals data entropy plus path KL to oracle reverse process, with optimizer being conditional expectation of true reverse jump rate.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对离散扩散模型，严格推导了连续时间马尔可夫链的ELBO，并证明了Oracle Distance定理：负ELBO等于数据熵加上从理论最优反转过程到学习过程的路径KL散度。该定理的唯一优化器是给定当前噪声状态下的真实反转跳跃率的条件期望。这一结果统一了离散扩散模型的学习目标，揭示了桥接插件预测器的本质。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对离散扩散模型，严格推导了连续时间马尔可夫链的ELBO，并证明了Oracle Distance定理：负ELBO等于数据熵加上从理论最优反转过程到学习过程的路径KL散度。该定理的唯一优化器是给定当前噪声状态下的真实反转跳跃率的条件期望。这一结果统一了离散扩散模型的学习目标，揭示了桥接插件预测器的本质。

quick_skim.title: Wasserstein Residuals: Learning Gradient Flows from Population Dynamics
quick_skim.abstract: Reconstructing population dynamics is a central problem in the physical and data sciences. Often, the dynamics are modeled as a Wasserstein gradient flow (WGF): a curve of distributions driven by an energy functional. Though there are multiple mathematical characterizations of a WGF, the dominant algorithmic approach relies on the Jordan--Kinderlehrer--Otto (JKO) scheme. JKO-based methods are inflexible to time discretisation and require solving costly optimal transport problems. We take a residual approach, enforcing the continuity equations via a non-negative loss function whose minimum is the WGF. Combined with a data-fitting divergence, this gives a single global objective. This perspective unifies several existing methods and leads to a new particle-based method, stitching, that is simulation-free and robust to large gaps between observations. We demonstrate that the stitching method achieves state-of-the-art performance across trajectory inference benchmarks. For code see github.com/BasisResearch/wasserstein-residuals.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Wasserstein gradient flow and optimal transport for learning distribution dynamics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: Wasserstein梯度流和最优输运用于学习分布动力学
quick_skim.llm_evidence: Wasserstein梯度流和最优输运用于学习分布动力学
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: Wasserstein梯度流和最优输运用于学习分布动力学
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a residual approach for Wasserstein gradient flow, unifying optimal transport and continuity equations, applicable to distribution dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对分布动力学重建问题，提出了一种基于残差的方法来学习Wasserstein梯度流，通过最小化连续性方程损失替代传统的JKO方案，避免了昂贵的最优输运计算。该方法统一了多种现有方法，并实现了更灵活的时间离散化。研究为最优输运在分布动力学中的应用提供了新视角，可能为扩散桥构建提供基础工具。
